input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>A static title card displays a cartoon orange bunny wearing a teal beret holding a pencil and paintbrush. Text reads "bunny + me" with a pink "show" tag against a mint green argyle background. Small white marks accent the circle. White dashed circles surround the central logo. No movement occurs during this interval.</think><answer></answer>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] A title card displays an orange bunny in a teal beret holding art tools with text "bunny + me" and a pink "show" tag inside white dashed circles on a mint green argyle background. [2-6] The scene shifts to a light yellow wood-grain background where a pointing hand appears, followed by two open hands and a hand making a rock-on gesture. [6-8] Hands vanish; a winking girl with short grey hair in a blue top and yellow skirt appears on the right. [8-10] A male character with long blonde hair in a ponytail wearing a pink outfit appears on the left with blue text "What is he wearing?!!" pointing to him, while a red circle highlights the girl's waist. [10-14] Red circles appear around the male character's hip pockets, and the scene remains static with both characters and annotations visible against the wood background. [14-18] A white banner reading "BASIC TUTORIAL How to Draw HANDS" overlays the scene where characters have red circles around their waists and text "What is he wearing?!!" is visible. [18-20] A cream "Creator's Note" appears, explaining the video is a lighthearted guide, signed "xoxo" with a bunny. [20-26] The book "BURNE HOGARTH DYNAMIC DRAWING

[80-82] A static black hand outline remains on the left while thick black ink lines are drawn over light blue guidelines on the right, outlining a thumb and fingers. [82-84] Two completed black hand outlines stay at the top of the white paper as a new light blue sketch, consisting of a horizontal oval with intersecting lines, appears below to form a base for a third hand. [84-86] Thick black ink lines are drawn over the bottom light blue sketch, outlining fingers extending leftward from an oval palm shape to create the third hand. [86-88] The third hand sketch is now fully outlined in black ink, completing the set of three hand examples with light blue guidelines visible underneath, while the pink bunny eraser and drawing tools remain stationary on the wooden desk.

[88-92] All three hand drawings, previously outlined in thick black ink over light blue construction lines, disappear from the page, leaving the white paper completely blank. The pink bunny-shaped eraser on the left and the grey marker with pink pencil on the right remain fixed in their positions on the wooden table. [92-94] A face sketch labeled "Realistic" appears in the top left, and a simple light blue circle sketch appears in the center of the paper. [94-96] The light blue circle sketch expands as four vertical lines extend upward from its top edge and a diagonal line protrudes from the left side, forming a basic structural outline of a hand with fingers and a thumb.

[96-98] A curved light blue line unifies four vertical finger strokes in the central sketch. The diagonal thumb line and palm oval persist. Static tools (pink bunny eraser, grey marker, pink pencil) remain on the wooden desk. [98-100] A horizontal blue line crosses the finger section. A red curved arrow points to it with text "Fingers bend here!" in dark red font. Tools stay stationary. [100-102] The central sketch (blue lines, red arrow, text) vanishes, leaving blank white paper. The pink bunny eraser (left) and grey marker with pink pencil (right) remain on the desk. [102-104] A maroon rectangular banner with white text "Match your styles" appears centrally. The pink bunny eraser and grey marker/pink pencil pair remain stationary on the wood-grain surface.

[104-106] The maroon banner displaying "Match your styles" vanishes, returning the white sheet to a blank state while the pink bunny eraser and grey marker with pink pencil stay stationary. [106-108] A detailed male face sketch labeled "Realistic" appears top-left, and a faint light blue circular outline starts forming bottom-left. [108-110] Below the static "Realistic" face, a light blue hand construction sketch evolves into a pointing hand. [110-112] The hand lines are traced with black ink, and a new head sketch labeled "Cartoony" appears to the right.

recent_thinks: [58-60] The completed black hand outline vanishes completely, resetting the white paper to a blank state. Immediately after, a new, single curved blue line appears in the center, beginning a fresh sketch while the pink eraser and drawing tools remain stationary on the wooden desk.

[60-62] The light blue sketch expands from a single curve into a rounded, cap-like shape. A thick pink arc briefly highlights the top edge. Subsequently, a horizontal blue line divides the shape, and a curved stroke extends from the lower left to form a thumb, establishing the hand's basic structure.

[70-72] A light blue sketch emerges in the upper-left corner of the white paper. This new drawing features a tilted oval shape intersected by a curved line, resembling a basic construction guide. The pink bunny eraser and drawing tools on the desk remain stationary.

[112-114] Below the "Cartoony" face, black ink lines trace the light blue hand sketch, defining the thumb and extended index finger. This creates a pointing gesture similar to the realistic hand on the left. The "Realistic" face and completed hand drawing remain static on the white paper.

queries.question: After the inking process shown in the earlier clip, what state is the hand drawing in? A. A blue circle sketch B. A complete black ink outline of a hand C. A realistic face portrait D. A clenched fist reference photo

samples.output: <think>Beneath the "Cartoony" head sketch, the hand drawing is finalized as thick black ink traces over the remaining light blue construction lines. The fingers and palm are fully outlined, completing the pointing gesture. The "Realistic" face and hand on the left, along with the stationary art tools, remain unchanged on the wooden surface.</think><answer></answer>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text:
[0-2] A title card displays an orange bunny in a teal beret holding art tools with text "bunny + me" and a pink "show" tag inside white dashed circles on a mint green argyle background.
[2-6] The scene shifts to a light yellow wood-grain background where a pointing hand appears, followed by two open hands and a hand making a rock-on gesture.
[6-8] Hands vanish; a winking girl with short grey hair in a blue top and yellow skirt appears on the right.
[8-10] A male character with long blonde hair in a ponytail wearing a pink outfit appears on the left with blue text "What is he wearing?!!" pointing to him, while a red circle highlights the girl's waist.
[10-14] Red circles appear around the male character's hip pockets, and the scene remains static with both characters and annotations visible against the wood background.

[14-18] A white banner reading "BASIC TUTORIAL How to Draw HANDS" overlays the scene where characters have red circles around their waists and text "What is he wearing?!!" is visible. [18-20] A cream "Creator's Note" appears, explaining the video is a lighthearted guide, signed "xoxo" with a bunny. [20-26] The book "BURNE HOGARTH DYNAMIC DRAWING SERIES DRAWING DYNAMIC HANDS" and a pink box "Recommendation for SERIOUS drawing books linked in description box!" appear next to the note. [26-28] The screen clears to show a maroon banner "Anatomy of a Hand".

[28-38] A cartoon right hand with a thick white outline appears against a wood grain background. Annotations sequentially label the 'Palm' with a red circle and text, the 'Thumb' with a green highlight and text, and the 'Fingers' with blue highlights and text. [38-44] The scene shifts to a white sheet of paper on a wooden desk, flanked by a pink bunny eraser and drawing tools. A light blue line is drawn, starting as an arch and extending into a tall, vertical oval shape in the center of the page.

[44-50] A light blue vertical oval centered on white paper is divided by a horizontal line. A short segment extends from the lower-left edge, and three vertical lines are added within the top section. [50-56] A thick black line outlines the thumb, followed by the index and middle fingers. [56-58] Thick black ink outlines the ring and pinky fingers, joining the previously drawn digits, while the right side and bottom curve of the palm are traced to complete the hand's outer contour over the light blue sketch. Throughout, a pink bunny eraser and drawing tools remain stationary on the wooden desk surface surrounding the paper.

recent_thinks: [58-60] The completed black hand outline vanishes completely, resetting the white paper to a blank state. Immediately after, a new, single curved blue line appears in the center, beginning a fresh sketch while the pink eraser and drawing tools remain stationary on the wooden desk.

[60-62] The light blue sketch expands from a single curve into a rounded, cap-like shape. A thick pink arc briefly highlights the top edge. Subsequently, a horizontal blue line divides the shape, and a curved stroke extends from the lower left to form a thumb, establishing the hand's basic structure.

queries.question: What color is the font used for the text 'How to Draw HANDS' on the title overlay? A. Blue B. White C. Red D. Pink

samples.output: <think>Thick black ink lines appear over the light blue sketch, outlining the thumb on the left and forming the index and middle fingers extending upwards. The underlying blue guidelines for the palm and remaining fingers remain visible on the white paper, surrounded by the stationary pink eraser and drawing tools.</think><answer></answer>
